name: evtx
summary: A cross-platform parser for the Windows XML EventLog format

documentation.readme: A cross-platform parser for the Windows XML EventLog format
usage_sections: Features: - 🔒 Implemented using 100% safe rust - and works on all platforms supported by rust (that have stdlib). - ⚡ Fast - see benchmarks below. It's faster than any other implementation by order(s) of magnitude! - 🚀 Multi-threaded. - ✨ Supports XML and JSON outputs, both being directly

arguments.name: --callers-n
arguments.description: Top N immediate callers per hot leaf

arguments.name: --label
arguments.description: Label used in output filenames (e.g. rust, zig)

arguments.name: --lengths
arguments.description: Comma-separated UTF-16 unit lengths

arguments.name: --out
arguments.description: Output dataset path

arguments.name: --out-dir
arguments.description: Output directory for markdown tables

arguments.name: --profile
arguments.description: Path to samply profile JSON (.json or .json.gz)

arguments.name: --syms
arguments.description: Path to samply symbols sidecar (.syms.json)

arguments.name: --top-n
arguments.description: Top N functions to output for leaf/inclusive tables

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: A cross-platform parser for the Windows XML EventLog format.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: A cross-platform parser for the Windows XML EventLog format.
documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `evtx` from its upstream repository, then run `evtx --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags. A cross-platform parser for the Windows XML EventLog format.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Penetration Testing, use evtx because A cross-platform parser for the Windows XML EventLog format.
When chaining Penetration Testing tools, pair evtx with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `evtx --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
